device_name: syngo.via MI Workflows; Scenium; syngo MBF
intended_use: syngo.via molecular imaging (MI) workflows comprise medical diagnostic applications for viewing, manipulation, quantification, analysis and comparison of medical images from single or multiple imaging
intended_use_full: syngo.via molecular imaging (MI) workflows comprise medical diagnostic applications for viewing, manipulation, quantification, analysis and comparison of medical images from single or multiple imaging modalities with one or more time-points. These workflows support functional data, such as positron emission tomography (PET) or nuclear medicine (NM), as well as anatomical datasets, such as computed tomography (CT) or magnetic resonance (MR). syngo.via MI workflows can perform harmonization of SUV (PET) across different PET systems or different PET reconstruction methods. syngo.via MI workflows are intended to be utilized by appropriately trained health care professionals to aid in the management of diseases, including those associated with oncology, cardiology, neurology, and organ function.
target_condition: oncology, cardiology, neurology, and organ function diseases; also supports radiotherapy treatment planning
ground_truth: For lung segmentation: independent cohort not part of training/tuning. For liver reference region: semi-automatic VOI positioning by two expert readers used as reference standard; expert reader identification of suspicious tracer uptake masks.
other_metrics: Lung lobe segmentation: Dice coefficient (DSC) higher for each lobe in subject device than predicate device. Liver segmentation: average Dice coefficient >0.8 and average symmetric surface distance (ASSD) <10 mm. PERCIST Liver Reference Region: subject device had 4 intersections with suspicious uptake vs predicate device 13 intersections (out of 129 subjects).
